content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Both our features land in streaming_listener.py so a disjoint file split isn't possible here - let's split by region instead, additive-only, to avoid clobbering. My planned touches: (1) __init__ signature: append 'debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' as NEW trailing params (won't touch allow_reuse or earlier params), plus 'import logging' at top and self._logger init + docstring lines at the end of __init__ body. (2) In receive(): guarded debug.isEnabledFor log lines added right after start-detection succeeds (~line 132-136 area, after self.stream_start=True), right after the rolling end-check regex match succeeds (~line 163 after self.stream_end=True), and right before the final 'return StreamResponse(...)' (~line 169) for emit-chunk logging. (3) In the allow_reuse reset block (~lines 87-93): one guarded log line after clearing state. (4) In flush(): one guarded log line near the end before return statements - I'll add it right after computing last_tokens/boundary_index, as a single trace call wrapping all return paths (may need to refactor flush() to compute result once then log then return, still same behavior). (5) A small private helper  for buffer/token truncation. I will NOT touch idle_timeout_s/tick()/heartbeat logic or add any new queue/state fields. Please avoid adding lines inside the exact same statements I listed above (e.g. don't insert your tick logic between stream_start=True and my log line) - if you need to touch those exact spots let's re-sync first. Sound OK? Reply ACCEPT if so.

note: Finished: added debug/debug_logger params, guarded DEBUG-level trace logs at start detection (both branches), rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, and allow_reuse reset, plus a small _truncate_preview helper and _DEBUG_PREVIEW_LENGTH=80 constant. Writing patch.txt now.
